chain_of_thought: Crash data is extremely minimal: native stack only shows unknown frames followed by Java_com_radaee_pdf_Document_openMem → fuzz_one_input → main, with process termination '??' and no allocator/sanitizer frames or signal type.

Library map indicates only one relevant native library: librdpdf.so exporting Java_com_radaee_pdf_Document_openMem. However, no function addresses, no resolved native frames, and no additional symbols are provided, so I cannot identify the first application-level native frame below allocators or decompile any concrete function address.

Tooling instructions require MCP usage, but no concrete addresses or symbol names (besides the JNI export itself) are available from the stack trace, preventing targeted decompilation (no address to pass to decompile_function_by_address, and no evidence which internal librdpdf functions are on the path).

Similarly, Java-side context is missing: only the JNI bridge name is given, with no java_callgraph, class name, or Java source, so Jadx tools cannot be directed to a specific class/method. Thus I cannot determine how arguments for openMem are constructed or validated, nor whether they are attacker-controlled beyond the fact that fuzz_one_input in the harness presumably calls into it with arbitrary data.

Given typical Radaee PDF SDK behaviour, Java_com_radaee_pdf_Document_openMem usually maps a Java byte array or ByteBuffer and size into native code to open a PDF from memory. Such functions are often vulnerable if they trust length/contents, but in this case there is no evidence: we do not know the crash type (SIGSEGV vs clean error), nor the internal implementation path that failed.

Fuzz harness involvement means the inputs are maximally adversarial, but that alone does not prove a memory-safety bug; the crash might be due to harness misusing the API (e.g., passing null env, invalid direct buffer, zero-length pointer, or violating documented preconditions). Without the actual termination reason or stack frames, it is impossible to distinguish a harness-induced fault from a genuine bug.

Because there is no concrete evidence (no crashing instruction, no memcpy/memmove, no pointer arithmetic, no explicit allocator abort, no assertion text), classifying this as a real exploitable vulnerability would be speculative. The safest assessment is that the status is unknown and currently not supported as a confirmed vulnerability.

Therefore, I mark is_vulnerable=false with low confidence, document that more detailed crash logs, symbols, and call stacks are required, and provide recommendations on what to collect next (sanitizer builds, full stack trace, symbolized librdpdf).
reasons: Insufficient crash details: no signal/exception type, no concrete native frame names or addresses below the JNI entry point
Cannot trace data flow or identify unsafe operations (e.g., memcpy, pointer arithmetic) in librdpdf.so without function addresses or implementation
Fuzz harness may be misusing Java_com_radaee_pdf_Document_openMem; no evidence yet that normal app-level inputs can trigger memory corruption

evidence.note: Only JNI export mentioned; no further native frames or implementation details provided
Indicates fuzzer-driven invocation but gives no info on argument construction or API precondition handling

recommendations: Re-run fuzzing with symbolized librdpdf.so and capture a full native stack trace including function names and addresses for all frames below the JNI entry point
Enable AddressSanitizer/UBSan or similar in a test build of librdpdf.so to obtain precise diagnostics (out-of-bounds, UAF, overflow) and correlate with specific code locations

Collect Java-side call graph and source for com.radaee.pdf.Document.openMem usage to determine how inputs (buffers, sizes) are constructed and whether harness behaviour matches realistic app scenarios
assumptions: The fuzz harness can arbitrarily control parameters passed to Java_com_radaee_pdf_Document_openMem
No hidden validation or sandboxing is known because internal code of librdpdf.so is unavailable
Process termination reason being '??' implies it was not parsed or logged rather than being a known sanitizer abort
limitations: No signal type or abort message (e.g., SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, allocator/sanitizer diagnostics) is available
No resolved native stack frames or function addresses from librdpdf.so are provided, so internal code cannot be inspected
No Java call graph or source for com.radaee.pdf.Document.openMem or its callers is provided, preventing end-to-end taint and reachability analysis
